intentional_test_features: Red herrings: a severe post-exertional crash with cognitive failure and 'aphasia' mimics stroke, sepsis and metabolic encephalopathy.
Important-not-to-miss: stroke must be screened (partner-assisted FAST); global symmetric slowing argues against it but focal features would change everything.
Reverse-triage test: transferring a severe PEM patient to ED can itself cause harm — over-triage is penalised here, not just under-triage.
Anchoring: 'known ME/CFS crash' must still be paired with an active stroke/sepsis screen before settling on supportive care.
Commission trap: unproven agents (LDN/NAD+/CoQ10/Wahls) presented as therapy; there is no acute pharmaceutical cure for PEM.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
